text: Multimodal visual information fusion aims to integrate data from diverse sensors into a single representation that maximizes complementary information while minimizing redundancy. However, extracting such information remains a significant challenge, particularly for infrared and visible images due to the substantial modality gap. Conventional cross-attention modules prioritize correlation, yet effective image fusion requires a deliberate focus on complementarity—or uncorrelation. To address this, we propose a novel Cross-Attention Mechanism (CAM) specifically designed to enhance complementary feature extraction. Our approach utilizes a two-stage training strategy: first, independent auto-encoder networks are trained for each modality; second, the encoders are frozen while the CAM and a shared decoder are trained to synthesize the final image. By leveraging the CAM, features from both modalities are integrated into a unified representation that amplifies unique information and suppresses redundant noise. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple benchmarks. The source code will be made publicly available.
